instruction: Write a package main HTTP handler that wires an http.ServeMux (standard library only, no third-party router) for GET /kv/{key} and PUT /kv/{key} backed by an in-memory store. Use the Go 1.22+ method+wildcard route patterns and r.PathValue. PUT returns 201 Created, GET returns the value (200) or 404 if absent. Expose newMux(s *Store) *http.ServeMux.

Write a package main table/flow test using net/http/httptest for a key/value HTTP service built with newMux(NewStore()): PUT a key returns 201, GET it back returns 200 with the stored body, GET a missing key returns 404. Standard library only.

Write a package main stdlib-only JSON HTTP endpoint: POST /echo decodes a JSON body with encoding/json, validates it, and responds with JSON plus Content-Type application/json; 405 for non-POST, 400 for invalid/empty input, 200 on success. Expose newMux() *http.ServeMux.

Write a package main test using net/http/httptest and encoding/json for a JSON POST /echo endpoint built with newMux(): a valid message returns 200 with the echoed value and correct length; an empty message returns 400.

Write a stdlib-only Go generic ParallelMap[T,R](items []T, workers int, fn func(T) R) []R that applies fn with at most `workers` goroutines and returns results in INPUT ORDER, race-free. Treat workers<=0 as 1. Use a jobs channel of indices, a sync.WaitGroup, and close the channel before Wait; each worker writes only its own result index so no mutex is needed.

Write a stdlib-only test for a generic race-free ParallelMap(items, workers, fn): squaring a slice with several workers returns results in order and calls fn exactly once per item (count calls with sync/atomic); workers=0 still processes every item.

Write a package main HTTP middleware system (standard library only): a Middleware type `func(http.Handler) http.Handler` and a Chain(h, mws...) helper where the FIRST middleware listed is the OUTERMOST layer. Include a Logging middleware that records method, path, resolved status code (via a statusRecorder that wraps http.ResponseWriter and defaults to 200) and latency, and a Recover middleware that turns a downstream panic into a 500 instead of crashing. Wire a /health route through the chain in main using an *http.Server.

Write a package main test (net/http/httptest) for the middleware Chain: prove that Recover turns a downstream panic into a 500, that Chain applies the first-listed middleware outermost (so it runs first), and that the statusRecorder reports the code the handler actually wrote.

Write a package main repository-pattern data layer (standard library only): a Store interface (Create/Get/List/Delete) over a User{ID,Name,Email} entity, plus a goroutine-safe in-memory MemStore backed by a map guarded by sync.RWMutex. Create returns a sentinel ErrExists on duplicate ID; Get/Delete return a sentinel ErrNotFound (matchable with errors.Is); List returns users sorted by ID for deterministic output. Add a compile-time `var _ Store = (*MemStore)(nil)` assertion.

Write a package main test for the in-memory Store: cover Create/Get/List/Delete, assert ErrExists on duplicate Create and ErrNotFound (via errors.Is) on missing Get/Delete, verify List is ID-ordered, and add a concurrent test that hammers the store from many goroutines so `go test -race` proves the RWMutex makes it race-free.

Write a package main context-timeout HTTP handler (standard library only): a TimeoutHandler(timeout, work) that derives a deadline from r.Context() with context.WithTimeout, runs cancellable work (a select on time.After vs ctx.Done() that returns ctx.Err() when cancelled), and responds 504 Gateway Timeout when the deadline fires before the work finishes — never blocking the request forever. Write the result on success and always cancel the context.

Write a package main test (net/http/httptest) for the context-timeout handler: work faster than the timeout returns 200 with the body; work slower than the timeout returns 504 AND the test must prove the handler does not hang past the deadline (run it in a goroutine guarded by a select with a safety timeout).

Write a package main users REST backend (standard library only) in one file: a User{ID,Name,Email} type; a Store interface + goroutine-safe in-memory MemStore (RWMutex, NewMemStore) with Create (ErrExists on dup id), Get/Delete (ErrNotFound), List (id-sorted); logging+recover middleware with a Chain helper; JSON handlers returning correct REST codes (201/409/200/404/204, 400 on malformed body); and NewRouter(Store) wiring an http.ServeMux with POST/GET /users, GET/DELETE /users/{id} via r.PathValue, wrapped in the middleware.

Write a package main CRUD HTTP test (net/http/httptest) for the users API built with NewRouter(NewMemStore()). Isolate every case: newAPI() returns a FRESH handler per test (no shared state) and do(t,h,method,path,body) builds a FRESH body per request (never reuse a drained buffer). Cover: create->201+echo; same ID twice->409; GET missing->404; GET->200; DELETE->204 then GET->404; DELETE missing->404; List->200; malformed JSON (truncated `{"ID":"1",`, not empty `{}`)->400.

Write a config package that loads server settings from environment variables with sensible defaults (Addr defaults to :8080, read timeout, max page size), parses durations and integers, validates the result and returns an error for invalid values.
Write a config package test proving Load() applies the documented defaults (Addr :8080) when the environment is unset, and that a valid override is honoured.

Write an in-memory store package with sentinel errors ErrNotFound and ErrExists: Create returns ErrExists on a duplicate id, Get and Delete return ErrNotFound when the id is absent, List returns items sorted by id, using a concurrency-safe map.

Write a table test for an in-memory CRUD store that builds a FRESH store per subtest so state never leaks: cover create, duplicate create returns ErrExists, get missing returns ErrNotFound, delete missing returns ErrNotFound.

Write a models package (domain types only, standard library only) defining MULTIPLE exported domain types in ONE file with JSON tags: an entity struct (ID, Title/Name, Status string; valid status from a fixed set) AND a separate Event struct {Type, TaskID string} for the event seam, plus an exported sentinel var ErrInvalid = errors.New and a (t Entity) Validate() error method wrapping ErrInvalid via fmt.Errorf on empty or invalid fields. EVERY type the file's spec names must exist here — other packages import and reference them (never omit the Event type).
Write a test for a models package proving Validate accepts a valid entity and returns ErrInvalid for a missing required field, and that a second domain type in the package is usable.

Write a package main bearer-token auth middleware (standard library only): Auth(token string) returning func(http.Handler) http.Handler that rejects requests without a valid `Authorization: Bearer <token>` header with 401 (constant-time compare) and passes valid requests to the wrapped next handler. Compose it as Auth(token)(mux).

Write a package main test (net/http/httptest) for a bearer-token auth middleware Auth(token) func(http.Handler) http.Handler: wrap a next handler ONCE, then drive the wrapped handler with ServeHTTP(rec, req) — never call the middleware with (w, r) directly. Table-test: missing header 401, wrong token 401, correct `Bearer <token>` 200.

Write the canonical layered-backend service package (standard library only): TaskService depending on BOTH a Store interface and an EventEnqueuer seam, constructed with NewTaskService(store, enqueuer) — constructors take every dependency explicitly. Create validates (ErrInvalid via errors.Is on bad status/missing fields), stores (ErrExists passthrough on duplicate ID), and enqueues one task.created event ONLY after success. List(limit int) returns at most limit tasks sorted by ID, limit <= 0 returns all. Unique IDs via strconv.Itoa.

Write a table test for a layered service with a store AND a fake enqueuer: construct with the project's FULL constructor signature NewTaskService(NewMemStore(), fake) — fresh service, store and fake per t.Run subtest. Rows carry wantErr AND wantEvents together: a valid Create enqueues exactly one task.created event, an invalid task (bad status) returns ErrInvalid via errors.Is and enqueues NOTHING. Use t.Fatalf so a wrong count stops before indexing Events[0], and only inspect the event when the row expects one. Also cover List with limit: seed 5 tasks in a loop with UNIQUE IDs via strconv.Itoa(i), assert limit returns min(limit, total) and limit 0 returns all.

Write a package main CRUD JSON router (standard library only) on http.ServeMux with Go 1.22 METHOD+pattern routes (POST /items, GET /items, GET /items/{id} via r.PathValue): NewRouter(token) wraps ONLY the mutating POST route in bearer-token auth middleware (register the wrapped http.Handler with mux.Handle, bare handler funcs with mux.HandleFunc), 201 echoes the created entity as JSON, a duplicate ID is 409 Conflict via errors.Is(err, ErrExists), a malformed body is 400, GET list returns a JSON array, a missing id is 404.

Write a package main table test (net/http/httptest) for a CRUD JSON router with auth on mutating routes: EVERY t.Run subtest constructs a FRESH router with NewRouter and seeds its OWN fixtures via an authenticated helper (a shared router leaks earlier creates into later cases — 201 becomes 409 and a missing-id case finds a leftover). Every POST sends the valid Authorization: Bearer token (only the dedicated 401 row omits it); decode the 201 echo into the TYPED entity struct and the list into a SLICE of the entity (never a string); duplicate create asserts 409 by seeding then reposting the same id; missing id asserts 404 on a fresh router seeded with a different id.

Write a goroutine-safe in-memory service for tasks with validation and filtered listing: Create validates fields and returns sentinel ErrInvalid on a bad status and ErrExists on a duplicate ID, Get returns ErrNotFound (all errors.Is-matchable), and List(status, limit) filters by status (empty = all), returns results sorted by ID for deterministic output, and caps at limit when limit > 0. Guard the map with sync.RWMutex.

Write a scenario/table test for a service List with status filter and limit over multiple seeded tasks (multi-entity CRUD). CONSISTENCY RULE: each table row declares its OWN seed fixtures and its wantIDs are derived from that row's seed alone — never from records another row or a shared setup created. Build a FRESH service inside every t.Run subtest, create the row's seed there, then assert the filtered, sorted, limit-capped result exactly.

Write a package main, goroutine-safe in-memory store backed by a map[string]Record guarded by a sync.RWMutex, where Record is a struct with a Hits int access counter. Get(key) returns the record and increments its Hits count, or a sentinel ErrNotFound (errors.Is-matchable) when absent; Peek(key) returns the record WITHOUT touching Hits, or ErrNotFound. The increment must persist so a later read sees it. Standard library only.
